task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function meets the specified requirements, handling the edge case of consecutive commas correctly.
trace: I used the `replace()` method to handle consecutive commas as a single delimiter. This was followed by the `split()` function to split the string, and finally I employed list comprehension along with an `if` statement to avoid empty pieces at the start and end of the final output. One edge case handled is when there are multiple runs of consecutive commas in the input string, as they would be compressed into a single comma within the output.
